title: Chapter III.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Epistle of Theonas, Bishop of Alexandria, to Lucianus, the Chief Chamberlain.
verses.text: But because, as I apprehend it, ye are assigned to different offices, and you, Lucianus, are styled the head of them all, whom, also, by the grace of Christ given you, you are able to direct and dispose in their different spheres, I am certain that it will not displease you if I also bring before your notice, in a particular and summary manner, some of my sentiments on the subject of these offices.

verses.book: The Epistle of Theonas, Bishop of Alexandria, to Lucianus, the Chief Chamberlain.
verses.text: For I hear that one of you keeps the private moneys of the emperor; another the imperial robes and ornaments; another the precious vessels; another the books, who, I understand, does not as yet belong to the believers; and others the different parts of the household goods.

verses.book: The Epistle of Theonas, Bishop of Alexandria, to Lucianus, the Chief Chamberlain.
verses.text: And in what manner, therefore, these charges ought, in my judgment, to be executed, I shall indicate in a few words.
